description: An object conveying information or instruction; to make known through gesture or mark. 'Kiroa' names what speaks without voice—the visible communication.
sound_symbolism: The word marks with permanence. 'Ki' begins with velar precision, the decisive act of making a mark. 'Roa' extends with enduring presence. A sign as something crafted to last, a mark that persists.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'kiroa' (the sign) - more communicative than 'siru' (marker): it conveys a message, not just a location. Showing and telling take their own verbs.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Signs point to meaning—the sign is not the thing itself but indication toward understanding.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Signs enable communication—shared symbols allowing understanding across languages.
